desc: Riga combines grand Art Nouveau boulevards, a lively old town, and Baltic urban culture, with dramatic daylight swings shaping winter and summer travel styles.
hls: Old Town lanes with strong café/bar culture
Huge Central Market in historic hangars
Fast access to Jūrmala beach and pine coast

todo.name: Old Town + Daugava embankment
todo.desc: Walk cathedral squares and riverside viewpoints to orient yourself in the medieval core.

todo.name: Alberta iela architecture route
todo.desc: Dedicate time to the Art Nouveau quarter and decorative facades around the Quiet Centre.

todo.name: Central Market tasting pass
todo.desc: Sample smoked fish, rye breads, pickles, and Latvian snacks inside the old zeppelin hangars.

todo.name: National Library sunset view
todo.desc: Cross the river for skyline views; in winter, plan this early because daylight ends quickly.

todo.name: Jūrmala half-day
todo.desc: Take the train for sea air and pine walks, especially rewarding from late spring to early autumn.

todo.name: Winter sauna + warm bars
todo.desc: In dark-season months, combine spa/sauna sessions with cozy cocktail or beer venues.

wifi.note: Very strong in hotels and city cafés; eSIM and public Wi‑Fi coverage are widely available.

prac.fltNote: Riga is a regional Baltic hub with frequent UK/European service and easy public bus connections to center.
prac.lang: Latvian (Russian widely understood in some settings)
prac.visa: Latvia is in Schengen; standard Schengen entry rules apply.
alerts: From late November to January, daylight is short; build itineraries around a 6–7 hour light window.

sweet: Best balance is May–September; December can be atmospheric for markets, but winter trips must plan around very short daylight.
